pages.raw_text:
The national grange, represent
ing 100000 farmers OF the United
States, has begun a campaign for
the improvement of public high
ways. Congress will be asked to
adopt such legislation as is neces-
sary to bring about a better con-
dition Of affairs.


At the last convention of the ma
tional grange, held recently in Con-
cord, N. H., the following resolu-
tons were adopted:


Resolved, that the national
grange favors general policy of
good roads construction by the va
rious municipalities, counties and
states, and


Resolved, that we favor the i'm
mediate enactment of legislation by
congress making liberal federal
appropriations for the improvement
of the public highways of the
country, these appropriations to be
expended in such manner as con-
gress may prescribe


The scheme outlined compre-
bends the expenditure of $50,000,-
900 in five annual installments for
the improvement of country high
ways.
